content: Hey guys what are Chaos Memories and Unforgotten Twilight?
hello i was wondering, what should i buy here i don't really know what's good or not and i have a bunch of credit to use here
I'm pretty sure its just the second posse of the turn
Second posse of a turn is Old Memories. I'm pretty sure this is what they're referring to when they say "Chaos Memories" or "Forgotten Memories"
I got my gmurphy to e3, should I just start saving pull currency now?
Unforgotten Twilight is what Chaos uses instead of old memories

> Posse Adjustment:
>     ○ The base Keyflare energy cap of the "Non-Chaos Realm" is increased to 2000, and a second Posse "Dust-Laden Memories" can be released each turn.
> "Forgotten Memories": Choose 1 from 3 random unlocked Posse to release. Each Posse can only be chosen once during exploration.
>     ○ When releasing the Posse for the second time under "Chaos Realm," it no longer releases "Chaos Memories," but instead releases "Unforgotten Twilight."
> "Unforgotten Twilight": Choose 1 unlocked Posse to Release, and make it take effect 2 times. Each Posse can only be chosen once during exploration.
i think their patch notes is MTL as well, so pretty sure "Dust-Laden Memories", "Chaos Memories", and "Forgotten Memories" are all just referring to Old Memories

Are the starter characters worth investing into
Ramona, Ogier, Lotan and Doll as an endgame team

Ramona is very good, Doll/Ogier are placed into comps who need weakness/vuln apply.
Ogier is additional shielding as well as STR gen, while Doll can heal and dispel vulnerable.
Lotan is more of a niche DPS awakener, who requires heavy investment to work.

to be clear, all DPS need heavy investment at endgame

Lotan is actually very good for what you would expect from a starter character. She is on par with e.g. Sorel or GDoll, plus she is cheaper to upgrade than other DPS and you get all her enlightens for free. Very worth building an endgame team around

The worst starter is probably Doll, as you would rather run most support characters over her

but as horizon said, if you need weakness for your 7th or 8th dtide team, you need weakness, and doll is there and free
Vuln dispel is also very nice since there aren't that many awakeners who can do that
And with her being invested and having her brain wheel, she can heal quite a bunch per turn.

Question: So I'm given to understand that 24 is usually run with an aequor team. What units would normally go with that? My current team is working alright, but I'm curious what changes I could make in the future.

As far as I'm aware, Aequor 24 is bad, however I'm not familiar with her kit to say that for sure. Either way, 24 requires heavy investment to work well, so unless you whale, I would prioritise building other DPS awakeners.
I wonder if I should try to build team while I’m building my aequor team

I'm kinda worried when this heavy investment moment is going to hit hard. So far I'm lvl 35 and haven't run into much issue getting what I need to build teams. What causes certain characters to require more "investment" and is there something I can save up now?
Or should I wait till I finalize the said arquor team before branching out

One team for story > many teams for d-tide.
I would invest in your main team if you are sure that this is the team you will be using for a while, push the story, get the resources, branch out for D-Tide teams.
Kk
Oops, so maybe making a new team for each Chapter was a bad idea, lol
It's either outdated kit or the need of big STR generation.
Outdated kit? Is that referring to like, power creep issues? Where older awakeners just can't compete as well as the newer ones?
Correct. Some awakeners' kit is outdated due to having no scaling on their kit or this scaling is very low.
However, most of the stuff can work with enough investment. It's just you can invest much less into other awakeners who can work much better.

so i'm making a list of what awakeners use what skill materials (lucky candy, dreamcatcher, etc.) and there's a few i don't know cause i don't have them (or maxed them, corposant <3 )
does anyone know the skill materials for Ryker, Hameln, Corposant, Miryam, Doresain, or Wanda?
It's all about investment and what you get in return after all

For example, Doresain. Arc 2 DPS awakener who is...not very good.
His cards can never kill. His exalt being his main strength. However that exalt scales off missing HP so it's either you kill a target or never kill it and just die.
Requires STR Gen and crits to have a chance to shine
And if you fail, you are dead
Some kits are just failed, that's it
https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/10UVim8m1SnY9fSDKfaH4AwnPPd22zsCDDNB6SV15yO4/edit?usp=sharing

You are welcome! Don't forget to use Frenzy/Emerge/Gluttony wheels within Arc 1! ~
Why those 3 in particular? They seem decent I suppose, but is there some secret?
The R I run consistently is Aged so I can get a posse at the start of every fight

Brown wheels are the most broken wheels within Arc 1. As for those 3 specifically, Gluttony gives you tons of hp (like 400-500 hp around Arc 8) plus some orisons choices and other 2 wheels allow you to grab much more artifacts from shops.

Ah, I didn't notice how Gluttony scales. So far I haven't had many health issues yet as I can typically end most fights on turn 1, but I imagine it'll get harder. Some of the event challenges from the Murphy Bedroom event made me use a revive.
yeah gluttony is a "if the game is too hard use this" but not worth getting otherwise, the 2 shop ones are good cause they let you grab much stronger relics

keyflare one can be nice if you don't have a proper keyflare setup yet, symptom removal at rest spot is good for the symptom achievements, innate rouse is good for some chars in some situations, can do a bunch with rare wheels in arc1

yeah no that's normal that's why it's a nice wheel when you get these, also lets you run into the symptom tiles and not care anyway
as you go up the chapters the game will get harder but they nerfed arc1 story a decent bit so it's much easier nowadays to get through the first time

Viable options:
- Kath (isn't playable before E3, so you will have to wait for Horizon Prototypes to make her work. However, she becomes one of the strongest hypercarries in the game at that point)
- Castor (serviceable at E0, but quite niche, purely a support, needs E3 to become DPS. Though I would choose Kath in that case).
- Corpo (niche at E0, wants E2/E3, E2 gives weakness and E3 gives vulnerable. Played with Aurita/Casiah in discard comp).
- Pollux (hard to play at E0, wants E3 and best paired with Castor, but since you can't grab both...eh...I'm not sure about Pollux teams outside of Castor one. I know the exist, but uh...he has some survivability problems without support)
I'm not looking at Clem since getting DPS > supports imo
I'm not familiar with Pollux much, but he requires some resources and a team built around him.
kk
Kath can solo carry all of end-game content, but needs E3
And that's her only needed Enlightenment tbh, her OE is meh.

Unless you like the idea of discard comp, I would choose Kath and slowly dupe her with Horizon Prototypes. Getting Castor is also fine, but he is a bit weaker than Kath in terms of solo carrying since he doesn't have AoE
And for a d-tide team you need at least Jenkins E1 for Castor

Okay
the best (general) set for keyflare generators

Some of the early game covs are some of the strongest in game
Burial Grounds - ch. 4 ish cov one of the best generic support covs in the game
Steppenwolf - viable endgame dps cov, rose scrip shop
Metamorphosis - viable endgame support cov, rose scrip shop
Crimson Heart - generic good dps cov for most characters
I wouldn't say Burial Grounds is necessarily there with the rest of them
because the use case is more niche
it's good don't get me wrong, but you use it on DR teams more than generally
Of the 4 I listed burial is easily the best and most generically good
I'm just wanting to start investing in some to make clearing 7 and hard mode easier

oh
i mean the 3-set bonus and 6-set bonus is... DR gain
i don't feel like that's good for arc 2
unless you play a full DR oriented team
of course you can get the stats to be crit / keyflare as needed
but the point of "which set to choose from" is those set bonuses no?

chapter 7 give you a good set for keyflare generators
chapter 8 give you a good set for burster dps (dps characters that want to setup and burst from high hp to 0 in 1 turn)
steppenwolf is good before then
level 58 keeper give you access to dream of medicine, arguably the best covenant for either keyflare generators or dpses

if you don't have access to the above you can try to invest in scarlet.... in the shop (i forgot the set name), which is decent for dpses
there are sets for aliemus batteries (metamorphosis) and sets for defensive awakeners (feast), but generally i think those 2 types are what you want as generic set at the moment
a life drain set for keyflare generators, and a generic damage set for dps

It takes 1 well built death resistance covenant to go up to 70%, one wheel to go up to 120%
That’s basically enough to rely on in endgame

is more useful
because veterans can proc DR and then regain the HP loss

That’s true but also all new players need to clear is 1 limited and a good wheel set up

moreover, for keyflare generators set, you generally want
Keyflare on Piece I
whatever on Piece II (Crit rate if that keyflare generator can deal damage)
Dmg Amp on Piece III
Keyflare on Piece IV
Keyflare on Piece V
Dmg Amp on Piece VI
as the mainstat
for the substat, reroll until you get a decent amount of keyflare regen
or if you can't afford it, reroll until you see useful stats and stop there
it's more important to upgrade the keyflare pieces to +12
for damage dealers, i generally recommend getting as much crit rate as possible, unless if you're building the chapter 8 set (in which case you want crit dmg)

which would mean
Crit rate on Piece I
Crit rate on Piece II
Crit rate on Piece III
Keyflare on Piece IV
Keyflare on Piece V
Dmg Amp on Piece VI
that's my recommendation, though 4 5 6 can be flexible (more dmg amp for example)
for substats, go for crit rate > crit dmg > keyflare = dmg amp would be my recommendation
after you get a good set for dps and a good set for keyflare generators i'd recommend filling 2 random steppenwolf to the other 2 awakeners until later
no need to +12 them even
they're fillers as they have no team unique passive
